metadata.document: DURApulse GS10 AC Drive User Manual — Chapter 6 Maintenance and Troubleshooting

content:
MIRA garage reference — AutomationDirect GS10 DURApulse VFD complete keypad fault code table (P06.17 records). Each fault halts the drive and latches until cleared via STOP/RESET keypad press or Modbus write of 2 to register 0x2002 (8194).

| ID | Display | Name | Common cause |
|----|---------|------|--------------|
| 1  | ocA | Over-current during acceleration | Accel time too short for load inertia; short on output |
| 2  | ocd | Over-current during deceleration | Decel time too short; regenerative energy; check braking resistor |
| 3  | ocn | Over-current at constant speed | Sudden load increase; mechanical jam; wrong V/Hz curve |
| 4  | GFF | Ground fault | Motor winding/cable short to ground; insulation breakdown |
| 6  | ocS | Over-current at stop | Output transistor fault; replace drive |
| 7  | ovA | Over-voltage during acceleration | Input voltage too high; regenerative back-EMF |
| 8  | ovd | Over-voltage during deceleration | Decel too short; load inertia high; add braking resistor |
| 9  | ovn | Over-voltage at constant speed | Input voltage spike; check upstream regulation |
| 12 | Lvd | Low-voltage during deceleration | DC bus drop; weak supply; brownout |
| 13 | Lvn | Low-voltage at constant speed | Input voltage sag; loose terminal |
| 14 | LvS | Low-voltage at stop | Input below P06.00 level |
| 21 | oL  | Drive overload | Output current above drive rating for > P06.03 time |
| 22 | EoL1 | Motor overload (electronic thermal) | Motor running above P06.13 thermal limit |
| 26 | OH1 | IGBT overheat | Heatsink temp above limit; fan failure; ambient hot |
| 49 | EF  | External fault input active | EF terminal driven; check external fault wiring |
| 54 | CE1 | Modbus illegal function code | Master sent unsupported FC; check Cmd value |
| 55 | CE2 | Modbus illegal data address | Register address out of range for GS10 register map |
| 56 | CE3 | Modbus illegal data value | Data value out of bounds for that register |
| 57 | CE4 | Modbus slave device failure | Internal slave error; power cycle VFD |
| 58 | CE10 | Modbus communication timeout | No valid Modbus packets received within P09.03 time. Check baud rate, parity, wiring, P09.04 protocol |
| 76 | F30 | Comm fault latch | Repeated comm loss; press STOP/RESET on keypad |

Clearing a fault: (1) press STOP/RESET on keypad, or (2) write 2 to register 0x2002 via Modbus, or (3) power cycle. If fault re-asserts immediately, fix the underlying cause before clearing.

Reading current fault via Modbus: read register 0x2100 (8448) — high byte is warning code, low byte is the error ID number above.

metadata.document: DURApulse GS10 AC Drive User Manual — Chapter 5 Serial Communications

content:
MIRA garage reference — GS10 Modbus RTU CE1–CE10 communication fault decode and field fix matrix.

When the GS10 VFD detects a Modbus problem on the RS-485 port, it raises a CE-class fault. Diagnose from VFD-side error first, then PLC-side ErrorID.

**CE1 (ID 54) — Illegal Function Code**
VFD received a Modbus function code it does not support. GS10 supports FC 01, 02, 03, 04, 05, 06, 15, 16.
Fix: set Micro820 MODBUSLOCPARA.Cmd to 3 (read) or 6 (write single) or 16 (write multi). Do not use FC 23.

**CE2 (ID 55) — Illegal Data Address**
Master requested a register the GS10 does not implement.
GS10 valid ranges: 0x0000-0x09FF (parameters), 0x2000-0x2002 (command), 0x2100-0x210F (status). Address is the value put in MODBUSTARPARA.Addr; Micro820 subtracts 1 before sending (per Rockwell help: 'Addr decreases by one when sending').
Fix: use Mike's MIRA register map — 0x2000=command(8192), 0x2001=freq setpoint(8193), 0x2002=reset(8194), 0x2103=output freq(8451).

**CE3 (ID 56) — Illegal Data Value**
Value is out of range for the register. Example: writing 100 (=100 Hz) to 0x2001 when motor max is 60 Hz.
Fix: clamp ST code to motor nameplate frequency (P01.03). For 0x2000 command word, valid values include 1 (stop), 2 (reset), 18 (run FWD = 0x0012), 20 (run REV = 0x0014).

**CE4 (ID 57) — Slave Device Failure**
Internal VFD error. Power-cycle the VFD; if recurring, the drive is failing.

**CE10 (ID 58) — Communication Timeout**
VFD has not received any Modbus packets within P09.03 window. Most-common GS10 fault during initial commissioning.
Field triage order (Mike's playbook):
1. Check baud rate match: PLC channel and P09.01 must agree (commonly 9.6 kbps → P09.01=9.6).
2. Check protocol match: P09.04 must equal Micro820 Serial Port parity/stop. P09.04=12 means 8N1, P09.04=13 means 8N2 — Mike's CCW config uses 'Stop Bits=2' so set P09.04=13 (verify against `plc/GS10_Integration_Guide.md` Section 1).
3. Check slave address: P09.00 on VFD must equal MODBUSTARPARA.Node (Mike uses 1).
4. Check RS-485 polarity: if no traffic at all, swap D+/D- at one end.
5. Check termination: 120Ω at each physical end of the bus (Mike has 110Ω installed — within spec).
6. Verify P00.21=2 (run command from RS-485) and P00.20 source (frequency command). Without P00.21=2 the VFD ignores 0x2000 writes silently.

metadata.document: FactoryTalk Design Workbench Help — MSG_MODBUS instruction

content:
MIRA garage reference — Micro820 MSG_MODBUS instruction ErrorID interpretation for the Micro800 family (Allen-Bradley 2080-LC20-20QBB, 2080-LC30, etc.). Returned in MODBUSLOCADDR after a MSG_MODBUS execution attempt over the embedded serial port (Channel=2) or plug-in (Channel=5-9).

**ErrorID semantic groups:**
- 0 = no error; transaction completed successfully
- 1-11 = Modbus exception responses from the SLAVE (the VFD responded but with an error)
- 16-30 = Local Micro800 setup/parameter errors (your LOCPARA/TARPARA is invalid before the message is even sent)
- 31-89 = Network / transport errors (could not send, could not receive, framing failed)
- 90+ = State machine errors (instruction sequence violations)
- 130-200 = Reserved for plug-in driver errors
- 255 = Default / uninitialized — the MSG block has NEVER successfully completed since program load. **The serial port driver was never loaded on the controller, or the Channel parameter points to a port that does not exist.**

**Common ErrorIDs during VFD commissioning (Mike's RESUME_VFD_COMMISSIONING.md observations):**
| ID | Meaning | Action |
|----|---------|--------|
| 0  | OK | continue |
| 2  | Modbus exception 02 — illegal data address from slave (matches GS10 CE2) | check TARPARA.Addr |
| 3  | Modbus exception 03 — illegal data value (matches GS10 CE3) | check write value |
| 16 | Bad LOCPARA.Channel | embedded serial = 2, plug-in slot 1 = 5 |
| 17 | Bad LOCPARA.Cmd | use 1,2,3,4,5,6,15,16 only |
| 18 | Bad LOCPARA.ElementCnt | range 1-125 for reads, 1-123 for writes |
| 20 | Local communication driver incompatible with MSG | serial port not configured for Modbus RTU Master in CCW |
| 55 | Timeout — no response from slave | swap D+/D-, check baud, check termination, check slave node addr |
| 90 | Channel busy with another MSG | reduce trigger rate or serialize MSG blocks |
| 129 | Modbus/TCP socket error (MSG_MODBUS2) | not applicable to RS-485 |
| 255 | MSG block never completed since boot | serial port driver not downloaded — see CCW Embedded Serial sync procedure |

**ErrorID 255 is the smoking gun for an unloaded driver.** If you see 255 on every poll cycle: the PLC program is executing the MSG block in software (poll_step counter increments), but the embedded serial port has no Modbus RTU Master driver bound to it. Fix: CCW → Controller → Embedded Serial → Driver: Modbus RTU, Role: Master → Build → Download (must be a CLEAN full download, not incremental).

metadata.document: FactoryTalk Design Workbench Help — MODBUSLOCPARA and MODBUSTARPARA data types

content:
MIRA garage reference — Micro800 MSG_MODBUS data type field reference for the LocalCfg and TargetCfg inputs.

**MODBUSLOCPARA (LocalCfg) — describes THIS Micro820 controller's side of the transaction:**
| Field | Type | Meaning |
|-------|------|---------|
| Channel | UINT | Serial port number. **2 = embedded serial** (Micro820 D+/D-/G terminal block). 5 = plug-in slot 1, 6 = slot 2, 7 = slot 3, 8 = slot 4, 9 = slot 5. Mike's garage build uses Channel = 2. |
| TriggerType | USINT | 0 = trigger once on IN rising edge, 1 = continuous while IN=TRUE. Use 0 for state-machine-driven polling; 1 floods the bus. |
| Cmd | USINT | Modbus function code: 1=Read Coils, 2=Read Discrete Inputs, 3=Read Holding Registers, 4=Read Input Registers, 5=Write Single Coil, 6=Write Single Register, 15=Write Multiple Coils, 16=Write Multiple Registers. Mike's MSG blocks use 3 (read VFD status) and 6 (write command word + freq setpoint). |
| ElementCnt | UINT | Count of registers/coils to access. Max 125 for read holding registers, 123 for write multiple. |

**MODBUSTARPARA (TargetCfg) — describes the REMOTE slave (the GS10 VFD):**
| Field | Type | Meaning |
|-------|------|---------|
| Addr | UDINT | Target register address (1-65536). **Decreases by one when sent over the wire** — to read holding register 0x2103 you set Addr = 0x2103 = 8451, not 8450. Mike's ST code reflects this. |
| Node | USINT | Modbus slave address (1-247). 0 is broadcast — only valid for write commands (FC 5, 6, 15, 16). VFD slave addr is set by GS10 parameter P09.00 (Mike uses 1). |

**Mike's working ST code template (from `plc/GS10_Integration_Guide.md` §5):**
```
read_local_cfg.Channel := 2;
read_local_cfg.Cmd := 3;          (* FC03 read *)
read_local_cfg.ElementCnt := 4;
read_target_cfg.Addr := 8451;     (* 0x2103 output freq *)
read_target_cfg.Node := 1;

write_cmd_local_cfg.Channel := 2;
write_cmd_local_cfg.Cmd := 6;     (* FC06 write single *)
write_cmd_local_cfg.ElementCnt := 1;
write_cmd_target_cfg.Addr := 8192; (* 0x2000 command word *)
write_cmd_target_cfg.Node := 1;
```

This instruction applies to L20E, L50E, and L70E controllers — the Micro820 2080-LC20-20QBB qualifies as L20E.

metadata.document: MIRA Garage RS-485 Commissioning Blocker — first-party field notes

content:
MIRA garage reference — "Connected Components Workbench (CCW) shows: embedded serial in the project and controller are out of sync" troubleshooting playbook. This is the most common blocker when bringing up RS-485 Modbus RTU on a Micro820 from a clean state.

**Symptom signature (Mike's RESUME_VFD_COMMISSIONING.md, 2026-04, garage rig):**
- CCW project tree shows yellow ⚠ on Controller → Embedded Serial.
- CCW status banner: 'embedded serial in the project and controller are out of sync.'
- Every Download attempt: TCPIPObject download fails with a non-zero error, which aborts the rest of the download — so the serial port config never makes it to the controller.
- After power cycle: poll_step cycles 1→2→3 in the user program (MSG blocks execute in software) but the VFD sees zero traffic on RS-485 regardless of D+/D- wire orientation (no CE2, no CE10 either — total silence).
- mb_read_status.ErrorID = 255 on every cycle (MSG block never completed; default uninitialized value).
- VFD display shows F60.0 (running on internal keypad source, no faults, no comm activity).

**Root cause:** CCW's incremental download is bundled. If any one object (commonly TCPIPObject for the Ethernet IP-config, especially when the controller is on a different subnet from what the project expects, e.g., project=169.254.32.93 vs controller=192.168.1.100) fails during transfer, the entire download is rolled back — including the serial port config you actually needed. The Modbus RTU driver is never bound to the embedded serial port.

**Fix procedure (in order):**
1. **Force a controller IP match before download.** In CCW: File → Open Project → Controller Properties → Ethernet → set IP to match what the controller actually has on its display (use the LCD panel or RSLinx RSWho discovery — do NOT trust the project default).
2. **Download IP settings ONLY first** (Controller → Download → check only 'IP Configuration' if available; otherwise download just the Ethernet sub-object).
3. **Verify connectivity** post-IP-download: `ping <controller_ip>` and `Test-NetConnection -ComputerName <controller_ip> -Port 502` must succeed.
4. **Now do a CLEAN FULL download** (Controller → Mode → PROGRAM → Download → All Objects). If CCW still complains about TCPIPObject sync, try the USB cable path instead of Ethernet — USB bypasses the TCPIPObject step entirely.
5. **Switch controller to RUN mode.**
6. **Verify the serial port loaded:** in CCW Online Monitor, mb_read_status.ChannelStatus should be non-zero (port active). ErrorID should change from 255 → 0 (success) or → 55 (timeout, indicates polarity/baud/wiring fault — separate problem from the sync issue).

**What does NOT work:**
- Re-opening the project and clicking Download again with the same broken TCPIPObject (same failure, infinite loop — Mike confirmed by repeating).
- Power-cycling the controller alone (the issue is on the project ↔ controller transfer, not in the controller).
- Rewriting the PLC ST code — the program is fine, the runtime binding is missing.

**After the sync resolves, ErrorID interpretation continues per `micro800-msg-modbus-errorid-table-2026-05-15` chunk.**

metadata.document: MIRA Garage GS10 + Micro820 RS-485 Wiring Reference — first-party field notes

content:
MIRA garage reference — RS-485 Modbus RTU physical-layer wiring between Allen-Bradley Micro820 2080-LC20-20QBB and AutomationDirect GS10 DURApulse VFD.

**Micro820 embedded serial terminal block (left side of controller, marked 'Serial'):**
```
Terminal    Signal               Goes to GS10 RJ45
────────    ──────────           ─────────────────
D+          RS-485 A (positive)  →  pin 8 (SG+)
D-          RS-485 B (negative)  →  pin 7 (SG-)
G           Signal Ground        →  pin 5 (SGND)
```

**GS10 VFD RJ45 RS-485 jack pinout (labeled 'SG' on front panel):**
| Pin | Signal | Use |
|-----|--------|-----|
| 1 | +EV | NC — do not connect (powered output, can damage PLC) |
| 2 | GND | secondary ground (already have pin 5) |
| 3 | SG- | RS-485 B / negative |
| 4 | SG+ | RS-485 A / positive |
| 5 | SGND | signal ground common (PRIMARY ground reference) |
| 6 | NC  | unused |
| 7 | SG- | RS-485 B / negative (duplicate of pin 3) |
| 8 | SG+ | RS-485 A / positive (duplicate of pin 4) |

Use pins 7/8/5 (Mike's wiring) — they pair physically nearest at the connector for the easiest pre-made cable mod.

**Termination resistor:**
- 120Ω resistor across SG+/SG- at the END of the bus (the device physically farthest from the master, electrically not bus-mid).
- Mike's bench uses 110Ω which is within ±10% spec and works fine.
- ONLY one termination per bus end (two ends = two resistors max). Extra resistors load the line.
- For a 2-node bus (1 PLC + 1 VFD with no other slaves), put the 120Ω at the VFD end across SG+/SG-.

**Cable type:**
- Belden 9842, Alpha 6412, or any Cat5e twisted pair will work for runs <50m at 9.6 kbps.
- Twisted pair MUST stay twisted right up to the terminal block; untwisted pigtails > 5cm cause reflections.
- Shield grounded ONLY at the master (PLC) end — never at both ends (ground loop).

**Polarity check:**
- If wired correctly but no traffic: SWAP D+ and D- at ONE end (not both — that returns to original).
- Symptom of wrong polarity: PLC reports ErrorID=55 (timeout), VFD shows no CE2/CE10 (it ignores invalid line-state).
- Symptom of correct polarity but mismatched parity/baud: VFD shows CE2 or CE10 within seconds; PLC reports ErrorID=2 (illegal data address) or =55 (timeout) depending on which side detects first.

**Bias network (failsafe):**
- Micro820 embedded port has internal bias resistors enabled by default — no external bias needed for 2-node short bus.
- Multi-drop > 4 nodes or runs > 30m: add 680Ω pull-up to +5V on SG+ and 680Ω pull-down to GND on SG- at the master end.

metadata.document: Modbus over Serial Line Specification and Implementation Guide V1.02

content:
MIRA garage reference — Modbus RTU over Serial Line protocol cheat sheet for diagnosing wire-level issues between PLC and VFD/sensor.

**Frame format (RTU mode, 8 data bits required):**
```
[Slave Addr 1B] [Function Code 1B] [Data 0-252B] [CRC-16 lo] [CRC-16 hi]
```
- Maximum frame = 256 bytes total.
- Inter-character timeout: <= 1.5 character times (T1.5) — bytes within a frame must arrive back-to-back.
- Inter-frame silence: >= 3.5 character times (T3.5) of idle line between frames. At 9.6 kbps, T3.5 ≈ 4 ms. At 115.2 kbps, T3.5 is fixed at 1.75 ms (per spec).
- Slave address 0 = broadcast (write-only); 1-247 = unicast; 248-255 = reserved.

**Standard function codes (the only ones GS10 + Micro820 commonly use):**
| FC | Name | Direction |
|----|------|-----------|
| 01 | Read Coils | Master → Slave |
| 02 | Read Discrete Inputs | Master → Slave |
| 03 | Read Holding Registers | Master → Slave |
| 04 | Read Input Registers | Master → Slave |
| 05 | Write Single Coil | Master → Slave |
| 06 | Write Single Register | Master → Slave |
| 15 | Write Multiple Coils | Master → Slave |
| 16 | Write Multiple Registers | Master → Slave |

**Modbus exception codes (slave returns FC with high bit set, e.g., 0x83 = exception for FC03):**
| Code | Name | Meaning |
|------|------|---------|
| 01 | Illegal Function | Slave does not support this FC (GS10 sees this as CE1) |
| 02 | Illegal Data Address | Register not present in slave map (GS10 → CE2; Micro820 ErrorID = 2) |
| 03 | Illegal Data Value | Value out of range for register (GS10 → CE3; Micro820 ErrorID = 3) |
| 04 | Slave Device Failure | Unrecoverable error in slave (GS10 → CE4) |
| 05 | Acknowledge | Long-running request being processed |
| 06 | Slave Device Busy | Try again later |
| 0A | Gateway Path Unavailable | Modbus-TCP-only |
| 0B | Gateway Target Failed | Modbus-TCP-only |

**CRC-16 (Modbus polynomial):**
- Polynomial: 0xA001 (reversed 0x8005), initial value 0xFFFF, no reflection of final result.
- Computed over Slave Addr + Function Code + Data.
- Transmitted little-endian (low byte first, then high byte) — common bug when implementing custom Modbus from scratch.

**Wire-level decoding tip (when ErrorID=55 and you suspect physical-layer):**
- Borrow a USB-to-RS485 dongle, run modpoll or pymodbus from a laptop with VFD as the only slave, baud=9600, slave=1.
- If laptop CAN poll the VFD, the VFD is fine — the issue is the Micro820 port or PLC ↔ VFD wiring (not the VFD configuration).
- If laptop ALSO times out, the issue is on the VFD side (parity/baud/slave-addr mismatch, or wiring at the VFD).

metadata.document: MIRA Garage RS-485 Commissioning Decision Tree — first-party troubleshooting

content:
MIRA garage commissioning decision tree — start here when a technician reports "the VFD isn't running" at the garage bench (Micro820 2080-LC20-20QBB + GS10 DURApulse + WEG 1HP motor).

Walk the tree top-down. Stop at the first match.

**Q1: Does the VFD display show a fault (anything other than F60.0 or current freq)?**
- YES → read fault code, jump to chunk `gs10-fault-codes-complete-2026-05-15`. Specifically:
  - CE1/CE2/CE3/CE4/CE10 → Modbus comm fault → also see `gs10-modbus-rtu-comm-faults-2026-05-15`.
  - oL / EoL1 → overload → mechanical or load issue, not comm.
  - GFF → ground fault on output → motor wiring problem, stop and check insulation.
  - Lvd / Lvn → low input voltage → check 240V single-phase or 480V three-phase supply.
- NO → continue to Q2.

**Q2: Is mb_read_status.ErrorID = 255 in CCW Online Monitor?**
- YES → MSG block never completed since boot. Serial port driver not loaded on controller. Jump to chunk `ccw-embedded-serial-out-of-sync-2026-05-15`.
- NO → continue to Q3.

**Q3: Is mb_read_status.ErrorID = 55?**
- YES → timeout, no response from VFD. Check in this order:
  1. Swap D+ / D- at ONE end (most common — see `garage-rs485-wiring-and-termination-2026-05-15`).
  2. Verify GS10 P09.00 (slave addr) = Micro820 MODBUSTARPARA.Node (both default 1).
  3. Verify GS10 P09.01 (baud) = Micro820 CCW Serial Port baud (both 9.6 kbps in Mike's config).
  4. Verify GS10 P09.04 (protocol) = Micro820 parity+stop (P09.04=13 → 8N2; CCW shows Stop=2, Parity=None).
  5. Verify P00.21 = 2 (run command from RS-485) — without this, VFD silently ignores writes to 0x2000.
  6. Check 120Ω termination at the VFD end across SG+/SG-.
- NO → continue to Q4.

**Q4: Is mb_read_status.ErrorID = 2 (illegal data address)?**
- YES → Micro820 is reading/writing a register the GS10 doesn't implement. Check MODBUSTARPARA.Addr:
  - 0x2000 (8192) = command word ✓
  - 0x2001 (8193) = freq setpoint ✓
  - 0x2002 (8194) = fault reset ✓
  - 0x2100-0x210F (8448-8463) = status block ✓
  - 0xP09xx (e.g., 0x0900 = 2304 for P09.00) = parameters ✓
  - Anything else → wrong; correct the address.

**Q5: ErrorID = 0 but motor isn't running?**
- Modbus is working; the issue is logic or VFD config.
  - Check P00.21 = 2 on VFD keypad.
  - Check vfd_cmd_word in PLC: must be 18 (run FWD = 0x0012) or 20 (run REV = 0x0014). Sending 1 (stop) keeps motor off.
  - Check vfd_freq_setpoint > 0. Mike's program sets this from the speed pot ADC.
  - Check that the conv_state machine is reaching state 2 (run-commanded) — see `plc/GS10_Integration_Guide.md` §7 Phase 4.
  - Check physical contactor Q1 is energized (audible click + indicator light).

**Q6: Everything looks right but VFD won't accept run command (P00.21=2, freq set, command=18, ErrorID=0)?**
- Check VFD External Fault input (EF) — if EF terminal is unwired and the parameter expects it normally-closed, VFD blocks run with an internal trip that may not show on display.
- Check VFD enable input (DI1 default) — some firmware revs require DI1 high in addition to Modbus run command. Set P02.00=0 to disable DI1 enable requirement, or jumper DI1 to +24V.
- Power-cycle the VFD after any P-parameter change to ensure persistence.
